title: Three-part Comedic Structure
author: Aelius Donatus
source: Commentary on Terence, 4th c. CE. Renaissance critics later inserted 'catastasis' between epitasis and catastrophe.

lineage.note: Roman grammarian commenting on Terence; divided comedy into protasis (exposition), epitasis (rising complication), catastrophe (resolution). The three-act idea in embryo, predating Field by 1,600 years.

stages.name: Protasis
stages.function: Exposition. Introduce the characters and situation.

stages.name: Catastrophe
stages.function: Resolution — for Donatus, the unraveling and outcome, not necessarily disaster.

applicability.limitations: Originally for Terence's comedy; the 'catastrophe' has lost its Roman meaning (resolution) and become 'disaster' in English. Confusing on its own.
